_comment: Worked example: an ultra-strict TypeScript MCP server using pnpm. Generate the manifest first, then Claude reads it and injects `units`: node gen-manifest.mjs --repo /home/me/Development/my-mcp-server --src src --out runs/mcp-server-review/manifest.json
conventions:
- pnpm. Gates: pnpm check:all (lint+typecheck+dead-code, zero issues) + pnpm test:run must pass.
- Dead-code analysis blocks PRs; tests count as usage.
- Errors: throw new Error(ErrorFormatter.toolExecution(name, error)).
- Logging: import { logger } from utils/logger.js. NEVER console.log (breaks MCP stdio).
- Schemas: import from src/schemas/index.js; do not redefine inline.
- Path-segment IDs: encodeURIComponent at every URL interpolation site.
- Config flows through the Config object; tools/services must not read process.env.
- Ultra-strict TS: no any, no unsafe casts, no non-null (!) abuse.
- Mechanical issues are already enforced by lint — focus on semantic defects.

gates.testSetup: Vitest. Run one file: pnpm vitest run path/to/file.test.ts. Tests write a throwaway config store via an env override — never touch the real store.
